meta.name: clojure
meta.title: Clojure Skill
meta.description: Clojure-specific coding patterns, inline def usage, code block templates, and namespace handling for Clojure development. Triggers on: **/*.{clj,cljs,cljc,bb,edn.mdx?}

original_definition.skill_description: Clojure-specific coding patterns, inline def usage, code block templates, and namespace handling for Clojure development. Triggers on: **/*.{clj,cljs,cljc,bb,edn.mdx?}

actions.name: “Use the repl” means to use the Evaluate Clojure Code tool f
actions.description: “Use the repl” means to use the Evaluate Clojure Code tool from Calva Backseat Driver. It connects you to the same REPL as the user is connected to vi

actions.name: If there is no REPL connection, ask the user to connect the
actions.description: If there is no REPL connection, ask the user to connect the REPL instead of trying to start and connect it yourself.

actions.name: Define functions before they are used—prefer ordering over `
actions.description: Define functions before they are used—prefer ordering over `declare` except when truly necessary.

actions.name: Use `clojure.repl.deps/add-libs` for dynamic dependency load
actions.description: Use `clojure.repl.deps/add-libs` for dynamic dependency loading during REPL sessions.

actions.name: Guessing creates bugs - Code changes without testing introdu
actions.description: Guessing creates bugs - Code changes without testing introduce errors

actions.name: Use the Create Clojure File tool with initial content
actions.description: Use the Create Clojure File tool with initial content

actions.name: After editing files, reload the edited namespace in the REPL
actions.description: After editing files, reload the edited namespace in the REPL so updated definitions are active.

actions.name: When Clojure code uses `(read-line)`, it will prompt the use
actions.description: When Clojure code uses `(read-line)`, it will prompt the user through VS Code.

actions.name: Ask the user to restart the REPL if it blocks.
actions.description: Ask the user to restart the REPL if it blocks.

actions.name: Among many benefits this keeps function signatures transpare
actions.description: Among many benefits this keeps function signatures transparent.

actions.name: Reload the target namespace and execute tests from the REPL
actions.description: Reload the target namespace and execute tests from the REPL for immediate feedback.

rules.description: Always stay inside Calva's REPL instead of launching a second one from the terminal.
Do not over-escape JSON arguments when invoking REPL tools.
rules.condition: json strings in repl tool calls

rules.description: Always align multi-line elements vertically in all data structures: vectors, maps, lists, sets, all code (since Clojure code is data). Misalignment ca
rules.condition: align data structure elements for bracket balancing

rules.description: Dynamic dependency loading requires Clojure 1.12 or later
Never edit code files when the REPL is unavailable. When REPL evaluation returns errors indicating that the REPL is unavailable, stop immediately and
Interactive Programming requires a working REPL - You cannot verify behavior without evaluation
When editing Clojure files, always use structural editing tools such as Insert Top Level Form, Replace Top Level Form, Create Clojure File, and Append

directives.description: Docstrings belong immediately after the function name and before the argument vector.
Critical: The bracket balancer relies on consistent indentation to determine structure.
Follow Clojure naming rules: namespaces in kebab-case, file paths in matching snake_case (e.g., `my.project.ns` → `my/project/ns.clj`).
Consistent indentation is crucial to help the bracket balancer.
Keep the `and` and `or` arguments on separate lines:
Prefer inline def debugging over println/console.log.
